content.description:
The storage layer wrapping IndexedDB operations with validation. Delegates actual persistence to the [bootloader](item://f761e023-da8d-492d-9933-a75adcc8d316)-provided backend, enabling [Nested Instance](item://c0c0c0c0-0037-0000-0000-000000000000) isolation via ID prefixing.

## Responsibilities

| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `get(id)` | Fetch item by ID, throws if not found |
| `set(item, kernel)` | Save item with validation |
| `delete(id)` | Remove item |
| `query(filter)` | Find items matching criteria |
| `getAll()` | Return all items |
| `exists(id)` | Check if item exists |

## Validation

On every `set()`, the storage module validates:

1. **Type chain integrity**: Walks from `item.type` up via `.type` references, ensuring the chain reaches [kernel:item](item://00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) without cycles or broken references.

2. **Code item name uniqueness**: If the item is a code item, ensures no other code item in the same namespace has the same name.

## Nested Instance Support

The bootloader provides a storage backend that handles ID prefixing:

```
?instance=abc → items stored/retrieved with "abc:" prefix
```

The storage module is unaware of prefixing—the backend transparently handles it, enabling true isolation while sharing the same IndexedDB.

## Key Implementation Patterns

- [Type Chain Validation](item://c0c0c0c0-0014-0000-0000-000000000000) — Bounded type chain walking, circular reference detection, validation rules
- [Cycle-Safe Traversal](item://c0c0c0c0-0013-0000-0000-000000000000) — Visited-set pattern used in `_validateTypeChain`

## See Also

- [Bootstrap](item://c0c0c0c0-0002-0000-0000-000000000000) — How the bootloader provides the storage backend
- [kernel:type-definition](item://11111111-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — Type chains and validation

Storage.name: Storage
Storage.description: Storage layer — delegates to the IndexedDB backend, with in-memory caching.

Storage.instanceId.name: instanceId
Storage.instanceId.description: Get the nested instance ID (null for main instance).

Storage.initialize.name: initialize
Storage.initialize.description: Initialize storage (no-op — backend is initialized by bootloader).

Storage.get.name: get
Storage.get.description: Retrieve an item by ID. Throws if not found.

Storage.delete.name: delete
Storage.delete.description: Delete an item from storage and cache.

Storage.query.name: query
Storage.query.description: Find items matching a filter object (key-value pairs matched against item fields).

Storage.getAll.name: getAll
Storage.getAll.description: Get all items (excludes nested instance items). Populates cache on first call.

Storage.getAllRaw.name: getAllRaw
Storage.getAllRaw.description: Get all items including nested instance items.

Storage.deleteByPrefix.name: deleteByPrefix
Storage.deleteByPrefix.description: Delete all items whose ID starts with a prefix.

Storage.exists.name: exists
Storage.exists.description: Check if an item exists in storage.

Storage._validateItem.name: _validateItem
Storage._validateItem.description: Validate an item before storage — checks type chain and code name uniqueness.

params.name: kernel
params.description: Kernel reference for code item checks
